title: Centaurea stapfiana
taxonomy: Centaurea stapfiana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Centaurea, which includes a diverse range of thistle and knapweed species. Centaurea stapfiana is native to the Middle East and Central Asia. The plant is often found growing in dry, mountainous regions with well-drained soil. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Centaurea stapfiana exhibits the characteristic flower head structure of this family.

intro: Centaurea stapfiana, also known as Stapf's knapweed, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as wound healing, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Centaurea stapfiana include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are used topically to treat skin conditions, wounds, and minor injuries.
